description: Question-led source-of-truth model for a GreenOps data input maturity assessment and reporting tool. Designed for derived scoring rather than direct self-selected maturity.

1.label: Initial / Ad hoc
1.description: Inputs are fragmented, reactive, estimate-heavy and not reliable enough for confident decision-making.

2.label: Managed / Repeatable
2.description: Inputs can be gathered and reused in a repeatable way, but coverage, precision and attribution remain inconsistent.

3.label: Defined / Standardised
3.description: Data sources, methods and ownership are documented and standardised across key areas.

4.label: Quantitatively Managed / Measured
4.description: Data quality, granularity, coverage and confidence are measured and controlled using evidence.

5.label: Optimising
5.description: Inputs are continuously improved, increasingly automated and embedded into operational and strategic decision-making.

1.label: Reporting only
1.description: Data is suitable mainly for broad directional reporting and high-level awareness.

2.label: Directional decision support
2.description: Data can support indicative prioritisation but not high-confidence operational decisions.

3.label: Controlled decision-grade
3.description: Data is sufficiently reliable for controlled operational and management decisions in defined areas.

4.label: Optimisation-grade
4.description: Data is sufficiently granular, timely and attributable to support continuous optimisation and stronger automation.

global_scoring_rules.primary_principle: Domain maturity is calculated from weighted question responses, not directly selected by the user as the primary assessment method.
global_scoring_rules.derived_score_logic: Calculate weighted average score per domain using question weights. Convert to integer maturity band using nearest band with upward rounding from .75.
global_scoring_rules.direct_manual_override_policy: If manual override is allowed, it must be optional, secondary, clearly labelled as assessor override, and the tool must preserve both calculated and overridden scores.
global_scoring_rules.confidence_guidance: Confidence should reflect evidence quality, consistency of answers, clarity of lineage and completeness of rationale.
global_scoring_rules.impact_guidance: Impact is a separate score reflecting materiality, decision relevance, dependency value and risk if weak.

domains.name: Asset inventory and configuration data
domains.definition: Assesses whether the technology estate is known well enough to support GreenOps calculations across cloud, on-premise, end-user and colocation environments.
domains.why_it_matters: Weak inventory data undermines embodied emissions, service mapping, lifecycle decisions, utilisation analysis and attribution.
recommendation_themes: Link assets to services, owners and lifecycle state

scoring.normalisation: weighted average of selected option scores (1-5), rounded to nearest whole number with .75 thresholds upward
question_override_rules: If more than 30% of answers are level 1, overall maturity cannot exceed 3 unless assessor override is applied.
If evidence coverage is weak across more than half of weighted questions, confidence should be reduced.
If assurance/lineage questions score 1 or 2, decision-readiness should be capped at directional support unless other evidence is strong and override is used.

confidence_logic.low: Sparse evidence, inconsistent notes, or material contradictions across answers.
confidence_logic.medium: Reasonable evidence coverage with some gaps or assumptions.
confidence_logic.high: Strong evidence coverage, consistent answers, clear ownership, documented methods.

questions.text: How complete is the organisation’s inventory coverage across relevant estate domains?
questions.evidence_hint: Inventory coverage report, asset counts by domain

options.label: Coverage is fragmented and many estate domains are missing or unknown
Core domains are partially covered but major gaps remain
Most key domains are covered using standard inventory sources
Coverage is measured and monitored across most relevant domains
Coverage is comprehensive, actively governed and continuously improved

questions.text: How specific is the asset data held for physical and virtual assets?

options.label: Only broad counts, categories or cost centres are known
Basic asset records exist but model/vendor/location detail is inconsistent
Key fields such as type, model, owner and location are generally available
Detailed product, configuration and service-link data is available across much of the estate
High-specificity records exist and are maintained at scale, including model/SKU and service dependency

questions.text: How well are assets linked to business services, applications, owners or platforms?

options.label: Assets are largely unlinked to services or owners
Some manual service or owner mapping exists for priority areas
Standard service/owner mapping exists for key environments
Service and ownership linkage is measured and maintained across most environments
Assets are consistently linked to services, owners, platforms and lifecycle context

questions.text: How current is the inventory relative to real changes in the estate?

options.label: Updated sporadically and often out of date
Updated periodically but with noticeable lag
Updated on a defined cycle with acceptable lag for most purposes
Updated frequently enough for operational decision support
Near real-time or highly frequent sync for material asset domains

questions.text: How well does the inventory support location-specific analysis?

options.label: Location is unknown or only broad geography is available
Site-level location is partly known
Site/region is consistently available for key assets and services
Facility, room, rack or cloud region detail is available where relevant
Location is precise and decision-grade across relevant hosting and device domains

questions.text: How mature is the method used to match inventory records to calculation-ready product data?

options.label: Manual, ad hoc matching with weak consistency
Basic matching rules exist using broad categories or heuristics
Defined matching logic exists for key product families and environments
Match rates are measured and exceptions are actively managed
Matching is highly repeatable, quality-controlled and supports product-specific calculations

questions.text: How transparent and auditable is the inventory lineage?

options.label: Source systems and transformations are unclear
Basic source awareness exists but lineage is weak
Sources, transformations and assumptions are documented
Lineage, quality and exception handling are reviewed and measurable
Inventory lineage is transparent, reproducible and audit-ready

questions.text: How well is lifecycle status captured for assets and services?

options.label: Lifecycle stage is mostly unknown
Broad lifecycle states are known for some assets
Lifecycle stage is standardised for key asset classes
Lifecycle states support refresh, redeployment and extension decisions
Lifecycle data is rich enough to drive circularity and refresh optimisation at scale

1.label: Initial / Ad hoc
1.description: Inputs are fragmented, reactive, estimate-heavy and not reliable enough for confident decision-making.

2.label: Managed / Repeatable
2.description: Inputs can be gathered and reused in a repeatable way, but coverage, precision and attribution remain inconsistent.

3.label: Defined / Standardised
3.description: Data sources, methods and ownership are documented and standardised across key areas.

4.label: Quantitatively Managed / Measured
4.description: Data quality, granularity, coverage and confidence are measured and controlled using evidence.

5.label: Optimising
5.description: Inputs are continuously improved, increasingly automated and embedded into operational and strategic decision-making.

supports: Broad awareness and high-level reporting for asset inventory and configuration data

does_not_support: Confident automation or tight target accountability

supports: Some controlled decisions in priority areas
does_not_support: Strong claims of precision across the whole estate

supports: Strong management, engineering and governance use
does_not_support: None by design; caveats should still be made explicit where relevant

recommendation_triggers.guidance: Prioritise foundational improvement in asset inventory and configuration data before making strong decision-grade claims.
Standardise and expand asset inventory and configuration data so it supports more controlled decisions.
Use stronger asset inventory and configuration data capability to drive optimisation and measurable intervention, not just reporting.

domains.name: Operational power and energy data
domains.definition: Assesses the quality, granularity and usability of operational electricity and energy inputs across on-premise, colocation and, where possible, cloud-related models.
domains.why_it_matters: Energy is the core activity driver for many GreenOps calculations. Weak energy data limits reporting, overhead allocation, optimisation and hotspot detection.
recommendation_themes: Replace spend-based estimates with measured energy
Increase granularity from site to rack/host/workload where justified
Strengthen attribution to services and workloads

scoring.normalisation: weighted average of selected option scores (1-5), rounded to nearest whole number with .75 thresholds upward
question_override_rules: If more than 30% of answers are level 1, overall maturity cannot exceed 3 unless assessor override is applied.
If evidence coverage is weak across more than half of weighted questions, confidence should be reduced.
If assurance/lineage questions score 1 or 2, decision-readiness should be capped at directional support unless other evidence is strong and override is used.

confidence_logic.low: Sparse evidence, inconsistent notes, or material contradictions across answers.
confidence_logic.medium: Reasonable evidence coverage with some gaps or assumptions.
confidence_logic.high: Strong evidence coverage, consistent answers, clear ownership, documented methods.

questions.text: What is the primary basis for operational energy inputs across on-premise and colocation environments?

options.label: Estimated from spend, floor area, contracted capacity or broad averages
Total site or provider totals allocated using repeatable averages
Measured site-level energy used with documented allocation methods
Measured site, hall or rack-level energy with monitored coverage and validation
Granular, timely energy data linked to services, hosts or workloads

questions.text: How granular is operational energy data in practice?

options.label: Portfolio or site total only
Site-level data for some locations
Site-level measured data across key environments
Hall, room, rack or cluster-level granularity in major environments
Host, device or workload-linked granularity where materially useful

questions.text: How much of the relevant estate is covered by measured or modelled energy inputs?

options.label: A small minority of estate is covered
Coverage exists for some priority sites or platforms only
Most material environments are covered
Coverage is high and measured by environment or provider
Coverage is comprehensive with clear treatment of exceptions

questions.text: How well can energy data be attributed to workloads, services, teams or business consumers?

options.label: Little or no credible attribution is possible
Basic attribution exists using broad averages or capacity shares
Defined attribution exists for major environments or service groups
Energy can be linked to services, platforms or workloads in many areas
Attribution is decision-grade and supports operational intervention

questions.text: What temporal resolution does the energy data support?

options.label: Annual or irregular only
Quarterly or monthly
Monthly and sometimes weekly
Daily or hourly in material environments
Near real-time or sufficiently frequent for operational control

questions.text: How quickly is energy data available after the activity occurs?

options.label: Often delayed by months
Usually delayed by weeks to months
Available on a defined cycle with acceptable lag
Available quickly enough for trend and performance management
Available fast enough for meaningful operational optimisation

questions.text: How location-specific is the energy data?

options.label: Location detail is weak or absent
Broad site or region only
Site or cloud-region level is standard for key environments
Facility, hall, cage or rack-level locational resolution exists where relevant
Location resolution is fine enough to support local optimisation and comparison

questions.text: How strong is the assurance and lineage around operational energy data?

options.label: Sources and assumptions are opaque
Basic documentation exists but auditability is weak
Methods, sources and assumptions are documented
Coverage, exceptions and quality are monitored
Data lineage is transparent, reproducible and decision-grade

domains.name: Water data
domains.definition: Assesses the quality, applicability and context of water inputs used in GreenOps calculations, particularly for data centre, cooling and AI-relevant workloads.
domains.why_it_matters: Water is often under-measured yet increasingly material. Weak water data limits reporting, site strategy, cooling assessment and environmental trade-off decisions.
recommendation_themes: Establish basic water reporting where material
Link water to cooling modes and site context
Bring in local water stress context

scoring.normalisation: weighted average of selected option scores (1-5), rounded to nearest whole number with .75 thresholds upward
question_override_rules: If more than 30% of answers are level 1, overall maturity cannot exceed 3 unless assessor override is applied.
If evidence coverage is weak across more than half of weighted questions, confidence should be reduced.
If assurance/lineage questions score 1 or 2, decision-readiness should be capped at directional support unless other evidence is strong and override is used.

confidence_logic.low: Sparse evidence, inconsistent notes, or material contradictions across answers.
confidence_logic.medium: Reasonable evidence coverage with some gaps or assumptions.
confidence_logic.high: Strong evidence coverage, consistent answers, clear ownership, documented methods.

questions.text: How available are water consumption inputs for relevant facilities or providers?

options.label: Water data is absent or rarely available
Water totals exist for some sites/providers only
Water data is available for most material sites/providers
Water inputs are broadly available and monitored
Water data is consistently available across material environments with clear exception handling

questions.text: How granular is water data?

options.label: No meaningful granularity
Annual site total only
Periodic site-level totals for key facilities
Facility/system-level granularity for key cooling environments
Granularity supports operational decisions by facility, system or mode where justified

questions.text: How well is water data linked to IT or cooling-related activity?

options.label: No credible attribution to IT or cooling
Broad allocation assumptions only
Defined allocation methods for major facilities
Attribution is reasonably aligned to cooling systems or IT-related demand
Attribution is decision-grade and supports site/workload trade-off decisions

questions.text: How much temporal resolution exists in water data?

options.label: Irregular or annual only
Annual or quarterly
Monthly as standard
Weekly, daily or event-based in key environments
Timely enough to analyse seasonal or operational changes properly

questions.text: How location-specific and context-aware is the water data?

options.label: Little location context beyond broad site name
Site-level only with no wider water context
Site or region is standardised for key facilities
Water data is assessed alongside local climate or water stress context
Locational context is strong enough to influence siting, cooling and workload decisions

questions.text: How quickly is water data available?

options.label: Months late or only after annual reporting cycles
Delayed enough to limit action
Available on a predictable management cycle
Fast enough for trend management and escalation
Fast enough to support active operational and planning decisions

questions.text: How mature is the method quality for water calculations?

options.label: Narrative assumptions or weak proxies dominate
Repeatable but broad assumptions are used
Defined method exists for water treatment in calculations
Methods are validated against facility realities and cooling design
Method quality is robust, reviewed and fit for decision-grade use

questions.text: How transparent is the lineage and assurance around water data?

options.label: Sources and assumptions are unclear
Basic documentation exists only
Sources and methods are documented
Quality and exceptions are monitored
Water data and methods are transparent and defensible

domains.name: Infrastructure efficiency metric quality
domains.definition: Assesses the quality and applicability of PUE, WUE, CUE, CER, CLF and similar metrics used to translate IT activity into total infrastructure impact.
domains.why_it_matters: Weak efficiency metrics can distort non-IT load allocation and make outputs look more precise than they really are.
recommendation_themes: Replace design or marketing values with operational values

scoring.normalisation: weighted average of selected option scores (1-5), rounded to nearest whole number with .75 thresholds upward
question_override_rules: If more than 30% of answers are level 1, overall maturity cannot exceed 3 unless assessor override is applied.
If evidence coverage is weak across more than half of weighted questions, confidence should be reduced.
If assurance/lineage questions score 1 or 2, decision-readiness should be capped at directional support unless other evidence is strong and override is used.

confidence_logic.low: Sparse evidence, inconsistent notes, or material contradictions across answers.
confidence_logic.medium: Reasonable evidence coverage with some gaps or assumptions.
confidence_logic.high: Strong evidence coverage, consistent answers, clear ownership, documented methods.

questions.text: What is the primary basis for infrastructure efficiency metrics such as PUE, WUE and CUE?

options.label: Assumed, design or marketing values
Broad provider or site values used repeatably
Documented operational values for key facilities
Measured operational values monitored over time
Decision-grade measured values with boundary discipline and context

questions.text: How well are metric boundaries defined and applied?

options.label: Boundaries are unclear or inconsistent
Basic boundary definitions exist but are weakly applied
Boundaries are documented and used consistently
Boundary application is reviewed and exceptions are managed
Boundaries are robust enough to support comparison, allocation and challenge

questions.text: How representative are the metric values of real operating conditions?

options.label: Metrics do not clearly reflect real operations
Values are repeatable but may mask operating variation
Values broadly reflect operating conditions in key facilities
Seasonal or operating-mode variation is understood and measured
Metrics are operationally representative and used with explicit limitations

questions.text: How granular are the infrastructure metrics relative to where they are applied?

options.label: Portfolio or whole-site only
Site-level only for most uses
Site-level with some facility-level nuance
Facility, hall or customer-relevant granularity where needed
Granularity is appropriate to the allocation or comparison being made

questions.text: How location-specific are the metrics and their interpretation?

options.label: Metrics are treated as generic across environments
Site-specific values exist for some facilities
Site-specific values are standard across key environments
Metrics reflect facility differences and local infrastructure context
Location-specific interpretation materially informs decisions and comparisons

questions.text: How often are these metrics refreshed or revalidated?

options.label: Rarely or only for external reporting
Periodically but not enough for management use
Refreshed on a defined management cycle
Reviewed regularly enough to influence operations
Refreshed and revalidated often enough for active management

questions.text: How much confidence is there that these metrics support credible non-IT allocation?

options.label: Allocation use is weak and largely assumptive
Metrics support broad allocation only
Metrics support standard site-level overhead allocation
Metrics support more credible customer, hall or platform-level allocation
Metrics are strong enough for decision-grade overhead allocation in defined contexts

questions.text: How transparent is the evidence and lineage for these metrics?

options.label: Opaque or provider-asserted only
Some documentation exists but challenge is difficult
Methods and evidence are documented
Evidence can be reviewed and challenged with reasonable confidence
Metrics are transparent, evidence-backed and defensible

domains.name: Embodied emissions data
domains.definition: Assesses the specificity, reliability and usefulness of embodied emissions inputs used for lifecycle, procurement, refresh and circularity decisions.
domains.why_it_matters: Embodied emissions are increasingly material. Weak inputs lead to poor refresh assumptions and weak supplier challenge.
recommendation_themes: Support reuse and refresh extension decisions

scoring.normalisation: weighted average of selected option scores (1-5), rounded to nearest whole number with .75 thresholds upward
question_override_rules: If more than 30% of answers are level 1, overall maturity cannot exceed 3 unless assessor override is applied.
If evidence coverage is weak across more than half of weighted questions, confidence should be reduced.
If assurance/lineage questions score 1 or 2, decision-readiness should be capped at directional support unless other evidence is strong and override is used.

confidence_logic.low: Sparse evidence, inconsistent notes, or material contradictions across answers.
confidence_logic.medium: Reasonable evidence coverage with some gaps or assumptions.
confidence_logic.high: Strong evidence coverage, consistent answers, clear ownership, documented methods.

questions.text: What is the dominant basis used for embodied emissions calculations?

options.label: Spend-based factors only or largely absent
Generic category or sub-category averages
Structured vendor averages or product-family proxies
Product- or vendor-specific totals from LCAs/EPDs for much of the estate
Product-specific lifecycle breakdowns with strong primary data coverage

questions.text: How specific is the embodied data to the actual assets or products in use?

options.label: Very low specificity
Broad category-level specificity only
Product family or vendor-level specificity
Product-specific totals are often available
Product-specific lifecycle-stage detail is routinely available

questions.text: How much of the relevant estate can be matched to embodied emissions inputs?

options.label: Only a small fraction can be matched
Partial matching for priority classes only
Most key asset groups can be matched
Coverage is high and measured
Coverage is comprehensive with active management of exceptions

questions.text: How well is embodied data linked to lifecycle context such as age, refresh status, reuse or end-of-life?

options.label: Lifecycle context is mostly absent
Some lifecycle context exists but is weakly used
Lifecycle context supports standard refresh and inventory analysis
Lifecycle context materially supports reuse, extension and disposal decisions
Lifecycle context is strong enough for sophisticated circularity and refresh optimisation

questions.text: How well does the organisation handle supplier-specific or location-specific embodied differences?

options.label: Not handled meaningfully
Handled only in limited cases
Considered in major procurement categories
Supplier/product differences are actively incorporated where material
Supplier/product/location differences materially influence procurement and lifecycle choices

questions.text: How current is the embodied emissions dataset relative to procurement and refresh decisions?

options.label: Stale and rarely refreshed
Updated occasionally
Refreshed on a defined cycle
Updated often enough for procurement and refresh planning
Maintained dynamically enough to support live or near-live decision processes

questions.text: How transparent is the lineage and evidence supporting embodied values?

options.label: Source quality is unclear
Sources are partly documented but difficult to verify
Sources and assumptions are documented
Data quality and provenance are reviewed and measured
Embodied inputs are traceable, defensible and auditable

questions.text: How useful is the embodied data for actual decisions rather than reporting only?

options.label: Useful mainly for broad reporting
Useful for rough hotspot identification
Useful for standard refresh and category-level choices
Useful for supplier challenge and product-level decisions in many cases
Useful for nuanced refresh, reuse, redesign and procurement trade-offs

domains.name: Carbon intensity and emissions factors
domains.definition: Assesses the quality, precision and governance of emissions factors used to convert activity data into carbon outputs.
domains.why_it_matters: Poor emissions factors can distort otherwise good energy or usage data and create weak location- or time-based decisions.
recommendation_themes: Increase temporal and locational precision where justified

scoring.normalisation: weighted average of selected option scores (1-5), rounded to nearest whole number with .75 thresholds upward
question_override_rules: If more than 30% of answers are level 1, overall maturity cannot exceed 3 unless assessor override is applied.
If evidence coverage is weak across more than half of weighted questions, confidence should be reduced.
If assurance/lineage questions score 1 or 2, decision-readiness should be capped at directional support unless other evidence is strong and override is used.

confidence_logic.low: Sparse evidence, inconsistent notes, or material contradictions across answers.
confidence_logic.medium: Reasonable evidence coverage with some gaps or assumptions.
confidence_logic.high: Strong evidence coverage, consistent answers, clear ownership, documented methods.

questions.text: What is the dominant basis for emissions factors in use?

options.label: Broad annual averages with weak distinction
Standard national or provider factors applied repeatably
Defined use of location-based and market-based factors
Region/supplier/time-period specific factors are used where material
High-quality factors reflect temporal, locational and methodological nuance

questions.text: How locationally precise are the factors relative to decisions being made?

options.label: National or generic only
Country or broad region level
Site, cloud-region or provider-specific where needed
Local grid or provider context is reflected in major use cases
Locational precision is decision-grade for major planning and optimisation use cases

questions.text: How much temporal resolution exists in the factor set?

options.label: Annual only
Annual with occasional period adjustments
Monthly or reporting-period specific
Daily or hourly for material use cases
High-frequency temporal resolution is available where it changes decisions

questions.text: How clearly are location-based, market-based and supplier-specific methods governed?

options.label: Methods are unclear or mixed inconsistently
Broad method exists but with weak governance
Method rules are documented and generally followed
Method application is reviewed and controlled
Method governance is strong enough to support transparent external and internal use

questions.text: How much of the estate or activity is covered by fit-for-purpose factor logic?

options.label: Coverage is weak and inconsistent
Some key domains are covered only
Most material activity uses defined factor logic
Coverage is high with measured exceptions
Coverage is comprehensive and actively maintained

questions.text: How quickly are factor updates reflected in calculations?

options.label: Updated rarely or long after relevance
Updated periodically with noticeable lag
Updated on a defined cycle
Updated quickly enough for management decisions
Updated quickly enough for location/time-sensitive optimisation

questions.text: How transparent and defensible are factor sources and assumptions?

options.label: Opaque or poorly documented
Basic source documentation only
Sources and assumptions are documented
Factor quality and exceptions are reviewed
Factor selection is transparent, defensible and easy to explain

questions.text: How decision-useful are the factor choices in practice?

options.label: Useful mostly for broad reporting
Useful for directional comparisons only
Useful for standard reporting and some management decisions
Useful for location, supplier and planning decisions
Useful for confident optimisation and policy-level decision-making

domains.name: Asset, workload, application and service usage maturity
domains.definition: Assesses whether the organisation understands whether infrastructure and services are being used efficiently and meaningfully, including VM-, app-, service- and AI-relevant usage signals.
domains.why_it_matters: Without utilisation and service usage visibility, GreenOps cannot move from reporting to avoiding low-value demand and optimising live services.
recommendation_themes: Link usage to services and owners
Include AI prompts/tokens/inference metrics where relevant

scoring.normalisation: weighted average of selected option scores (1-5), rounded to nearest whole number with .75 thresholds upward

If evidence coverage is weak across more than half of weighted questions, confidence should be reduced.
If assurance/lineage questions score 1 or 2, decision-readiness should be capped at directional support unless other evidence is strong and override is used.

confidence_logic.low: Sparse evidence, inconsistent notes, or material contradictions across answers.
confidence_logic.medium: Reasonable evidence coverage with some gaps or assumptions.
confidence_logic.high: Strong evidence coverage, consistent answers, clear ownership, documented methods.

questions.text: How much visibility exists into infrastructure utilisation such as host, VM, container, storage or GPU usage?

options.label: Very limited visibility
Aggregate platform-level visibility only

Granular visibility across key compute/storage/GPU environments
Broad and decision-useful visibility across material usage domains

questions.text: How well are application and service usage metrics captured?

options.label: Little or no app/service usage visibility

options.label: Weak or no linkage
Some manual linkage exists
Key services have owner and usage context
Usage is linked to service, owner and operational context in many areas

options.label: This is mostly anecdotal
Basic periodic review exists
Defined thresholds and review methods exist

questions.text: How much temporal resolution exists in usage signals?

Periodic reports only

questions.text: How location- or environment-specific are usage insights?

Insights distinguish cloud region, site, cluster or environment where relevant
Location/environment specificity is strong enough to influence placement and design decisions

questions.text: How mature are AI-specific usage measures where AI services are relevant?

Basic counts such as users or calls exist only

AI usage is analysed by model, route, use case or workload type
AI demand metrics are strong enough to govern routing, optimisation and low-value demand avoidance

questions.text: How transparent and defensible are the usage metrics and methods?

options.label: Weak transparency and little confidence
Basic documentation exists
Methods and sources are documented
Quality and exceptions are monitored
Usage metrics are trusted enough to support difficult operational decisions

1.label: Initial / Ad hoc

5.label: Optimising
5.description: Inputs are continuously improved, increasingly automated and embedded into operational and strategic decision-making.

supports: Broad awareness and high-level reporting for asset, workload, application and service usage maturity

does_not_support: Confident automation or tight target accountability

supports: Some controlled decisions in priority areas

recommendation_triggers.guidance: Prioritise foundational improvement in asset, workload, application and service usage maturity before making strong decision-grade claims.

Use stronger asset, workload, application and service usage maturity capability to drive optimisation and measurable intervention, not just reporting.

domains.name: Allocation and attribution logic

Link impacts to owners and services

scoring.normalisation: weighted average of selected option scores (1-5), rounded to nearest whole number with .75 thresholds upward

confidence_logic.low: Sparse evidence, inconsistent notes, or material contradictions across answers.

confidence_logic.high: Strong evidence coverage, consistent answers, clear ownership, documented methods.
